key_finding: None of the four attacks (crafted via gradients from our own CNN-based Supervisor) transfer well to ForensicsSAM, a Vision Transformer-based foundation model — far weaker degradation than the corresponding white-box results, consistent with known poor cross-architecture transferability of adversarial perturbations. SEAR, the most precisely tuned attack, shows the weakest transfer of all (-0.4%), suggesting narrowly-optimized attacks generalize worse than broader ones.
defense_mechanism_finding: Activating ForensicsSAM's adaptive adversary-correction mechanism (activate_adv=True) reduced F1 on EVERY condition tested, including clean, unattacked images (-0.065), with a magnitude comparable to or larger than the drops on attacked images. This indicates a general accuracy-robustness trade-off inherent to the mechanism itself, not a case of the defense actively misfiring against architecturally-mismatched attacks. This reinforces the importance of the adaptive, detector-gated activation the original authors describe, rather than unconditional activation, which we deliberately tested here to isolate the mechanism's standalone effect.
